contents: From HoTT Require Import Basics Types Truncations.Core.
From HoTT Require Import Basics Types Truncations.Core.
From HoTT.WildCat Require Import Core Equiv EquivGpd.

(** * Abelianization *)

(** In this file we define what it means for a group homomorphism [G -> H] into an abelian group [H] to be an abelianization. We then construct an example of an abelianization. *)

(** ** Definition of abelianization.

  A "unit" homomorphism [eta : G -> G_ab], with [G_ab] abelian, is considered an abelianization if and only if for all homomorphisms [G -> A], where [A] is abelian, there exists a unique [g : G_ab -> A] such that [h == g o eta X].   We express this in funext-free form by saying that precomposition with [eta] in the wild 1-category [Group] induces an equivalence of hom 0-groupoids, in the sense of WildCat/EquivGpd.

  Unfortunately, if [eta : GroupHomomorphism G G_ab] and we write [cat_precomp A eta] then Coq is unable to guess that the relevant 1-category is [Group].  Even writing [cat_precomp (A := Group) A eta] isn't good enough, I guess because the typeclass inference that finds the instance [is01cat_group] doesn't happen until after the type of [eta] would have to be resolved to a [Hom] in some wild category.  However, with the following auxiliary definition we can force the typeclass inference to happen first.  (It would be worth thinking about whether the design of the wild categories library could be improved to avoid this.)  *)
Definition group_precomp {a b} := @cat_precomp Group _ _ a b.

Class IsAbelianization {G : Group} (G_ab : AbGroup)
      (eta : GroupHomomorphism G G_ab)
  := issurjinj_isabel :: forall (A : AbGroup),
      IsSurjInj (group_precomp A eta).

Definition isequiv_group_precomp_isabelianization `{Funext}
  {G : Group} {G_ab : AbGroup} (eta : GroupHomomorphism G G_ab)
  `{!IsAbelianization G_ab eta} (A : AbGroup)
  : IsEquiv (group_precomp A eta).

goals.conclusion:
group_precomp A eta
o (fun g : G $-> A => surjinj_inv (group_precomp A eta) g) == idmap
group_precomp A eta (surjinj_inv (group_precomp A eta) f) = f
group_precomp A eta (surjinj_inv (group_precomp A eta) f) == f

(fun g : G $-> A => surjinj_inv (group_precomp A eta) g)
o group_precomp A eta == idmap
surjinj_inv (group_precomp A eta) (group_precomp A eta f) = f
surjinj_inv (group_precomp A eta) (group_precomp A eta f) == f

contents:
Definition equiv_group_precomp_isabelianization `{Funext}
  {G : Group} {G_ab : AbGroup} (eta : GroupHomomorphism G G_ab)
  `{!IsAbelianization G_ab eta} (A : AbGroup)
  : (G_ab $-> A) <~> (G $-> A)
  := Build_Equiv _ _ _ (isequiv_group_precomp_isabelianization eta A).

(** Here we define abelianization as a HIT. Specifically as a set-coequalizer of the following two maps [fun '(a, b, c) => a * (b * c)] and [fun '(a, b, c) => a * (c * b)].

From this we can show that Abel G is an abelian group.

In fact this models the following HIT:

<<
HIT Abel (G : Group) := 
 | abel_in : G -> Abel G
 | abel_in_comm : forall x y z, abel_in (x * (y * z)) = abel_in (x * (z * y)).
>>

We also derive [abel_in] and [abel_in_comm] from our coequalizer definition, and even prove the induction and computation rules for this HIT.

This HIT was suggested by Dan Christensen. *)
(** We locally define a map uncurry2 that lets us uncurry [A * B * C -> D] twice. *)
(** The type [Abel] is defined to be the set coequalizer of the following maps [G^3 -> G]. *)

Definition Abel
    := Tr 0 (Coeq
      (uncurry2 (fun a b c : G => a * (b * c)))
      (uncurry2 (fun a b c : G => a * (c * b)))).
(** We have a natural map from [G] to [Abel G]. *)
Definition abel_in : G -> Abel := tr o coeq.
(** This map satisfies the condition [ab_comm]. It's a form of commutativity in a right factor. *)

Definition abel_in_comm a b c
    : abel_in (a * (b * c)) = abel_in (a * (c * b))
    := ap tr (cglue (a, b, c)).
(** It is clear that Abel is a set. *)
(** We can derive the induction principle from the ones for truncation and the coequalizer. *)

Definition Abel_ind (P : Abel -> Type) `{forall x, IsHSet (P x)} 
    (a : forall x, P (abel_in x))
    (c : forall x y z, abel_in_comm x y z # a (x * (y * z)) = a (x * (z * y)))
    : forall (x : Abel), P x.

hypotheses.type:
forall x y z : G,
transport P (abel_in_comm x y z) (a (x * (y * z))) = a (x * (z * y))

forall x y z : G,
transport P (abel_in_comm x y z) (a (x * (y * z))) = a (x * (z * y))

goals.conclusion:
forall
a0 : Coeq (uncurry2 (fun a0 b c0 : G => a0 * (b * c0)))
       (uncurry2 (fun a0 b c0 : G => a0 * (c0 * b))),
P (tr a0)

hypotheses.type:
forall x y z : G,
transport P (abel_in_comm x y z) (a (x * (y * z))) = a (x * (z * y))

goals.conclusion:
forall a0 : G,
(fun
   w : Coeq (uncurry2 (fun a1 b c0 : G => a1 * (b * c0)))
         (uncurry2 (fun a1 b c0 : G => a1 * (c0 * b))) =>
 P (tr w)) (coeq a0)

hypotheses.type:
forall x y z : G,
transport P (abel_in_comm x y z) (a (x * (y * z))) = a (x * (z * y))

goals.conclusion:
forall b : G * G * G,
transport
  (fun
     w : Coeq (uncurry2 (fun a0 b0 c0 : G => a0 * (b0 * c0)))
           (uncurry2 (fun a0 b0 c0 : G => a0 * (c0 * b0))) =>
   P (tr w))
  (cglue b) (?coeq' (uncurry2 (fun a0 b0 c0 : G => a0 * (b0 * c0)) b)) =
?coeq' (uncurry2 (fun a0 b0 c0 : G => a0 * (c0 * b0)) b)

hypotheses.type:
forall x y z : G,
transport P (abel_in_comm x y z) (a (x * (y * z))) = a (x * (z * y))

goals.conclusion:
forall b : G * G * G,
transport
  (fun
     w : Coeq (uncurry2 (fun a0 b0 c0 : G => a0 * (b0 * c0)))
           (uncurry2 (fun a0 b0 c0 : G => a0 * (c0 * b0))) =>
   P (tr w))
  (cglue b) (a (uncurry2 (fun a0 b0 c0 : G => a0 * (b0 * c0)) b)) =
a (uncurry2 (fun a0 b0 c0 : G => a0 * (c0 * b0)) b)

hypotheses.type:
forall x y z : G,
transport P (abel_in_comm x y z) (a (x * (y * z))) = a (x * (z * y))

goals.conclusion:
transport
  (fun
     w : Coeq (uncurry2 (fun a0 b c0 : G => a0 * (b * c0)))
           (uncurry2 (fun a0 b c0 : G => a0 * (c0 * b))) =>
   P (tr w))
  (cglue (x, y, z))
  (a (uncurry2 (fun a0 b c0 : G => a0 * (b * c0)) (x, y, z))) =
a (uncurry2 (fun a0 b c0 : G => a0 * (c0 * b)) (x, y, z))

hypotheses.type:
forall x0 y0 z0 : G,
transport P (abel_in_comm x0 y0 z0) (a (x0 * (y0 * z0))) = a (x0 * (z0 * y0))

goals.conclusion:
transport P (ap tr (cglue (x, y, z)))
  (a (uncurry2 (fun a0 b c0 : G => a0 * (b * c0)) (x, y, z))) =
a (uncurry2 (fun a0 b c0 : G => a0 * (c0 * b)) (x, y, z))

hypotheses.type:
forall x0 y0 z0 : G,
transport P (abel_in_comm x0 y0 z0) (a (x0 * (y0 * z0))) = a (x0 * (z0 * y0))

contents: (** The computation rule on point constructors holds definitionally. *)

Definition Abel_ind_beta_abel_in (P : Abel -> Type) `{forall x, IsHSet (P x)}
    (a : forall x, P (abel_in x))
    (c : forall x y z, DPath P (abel_in_comm x y z) (a (x * (y * z))) (a (x * (z * y))))
    (x : G)
    : Abel_ind P a c (abel_in x) = a x
    := idpath.

Definition Abel_ind_beta_abel_in_comm (P : Abel -> Type) `{forall x, IsHSet (P x)}
    (a : forall x, P (abel_in x))
    (c : forall x y z, abel_in_comm x y z # a (x * (y * z)) = a (x * (z * y)))
    (x y z : G)
    : apD (Abel_ind P a c) (abel_in_comm x y z) = c x y z.

goals.conclusion: apD (Abel_ind P a c) (abel_in_comm x y z) = c x y z

hypotheses.type:
forall x0 y0 z0 : G,
transport P (abel_in_comm x0 y0 z0) (a (x0 * (y0 * z0))) = a (x0 * (z0 * y0))

goals.conclusion: apD (Abel_ind P a c) (abel_in_comm x y z) = c x y z

hypotheses.type:
forall x0 y0 z0 : G,
transport P (abel_in_comm x0 y0 z0) (a (x0 * (y0 * z0))) = a (x0 * (z0 * y0))

goals.conclusion:
apD
  (fun
     x0 : Coeq (uncurry2 (fun a0 b c0 : G => a0 * (b * c0)))
            (uncurry2 (fun a0 b c0 : G => a0 * (c0 * b))) =>
   Abel_ind P a c (tr x0))
  (cglue (x, y, z)) =
transport_compose P tr (cglue (x, y, z))
  (Abel_ind P a c (tr (coeq (x * (y * z))))) @
c x y z

hypotheses.type:
forall x0 y0 z0 : G,
transport P (abel_in_comm x0 y0 z0) (a (x0 * (y0 * z0))) = a (x0 * (z0 * y0))

contents: (** We also have a recursion principle. *)

Definition Abel_rec (P : Type) `{IsHSet P} (a : G -> P)
    (c : forall x y z, a (x * (y * z)) = a (x * (z * y)))
    : Abel -> P.

hypotheses.type: forall x y z : G, a (x * (y * z)) = a (x * (z * y))
forall x y z : G, a (x * (y * z)) = a (x * (z * y))

goals.conclusion:
forall x y z : G,
transport (fun _ : Abel => P) (abel_in_comm x y z) (a (x * (y * z))) =
a (x * (z * y))

hypotheses.type: forall x y z : G, a (x * (y * z)) = a (x * (z * y))

contents: (** Here is a simpler version of [Abel_ind] when our target is an [HProp]. This lets us discard all the higher paths. *)

goals.conclusion:
forall
a0 : Coeq (uncurry2 (fun a0 b c : G => a0 * (b * c)))
       (uncurry2 (fun a0 b c : G => a0 * (c * b))),
P (tr a0)

forall a0 : G,
(fun
   x : Coeq (uncurry2 (fun a1 b c : G => a1 * (b * c)))
         (uncurry2 (fun a1 b c : G => a1 * (c * b))) =>
 P (tr x)) (coeq a0)

contents: (** The [IsHProp] argument of [Abel_ind_hprop] can usually be found by typeclass resolution, but [srapply] is slow, so we use this tactic instead. *)
Local Ltac Abel_ind_hprop x := snapply Abel_ind_hprop; [exact _ | intro x].

(** We make sure that [G] is implicit in the arguments of [abel_in]
 and [abel_in_comm]. *)
(** Now we can show that [Abel G] is in fact an abelian group. We will denote the operation in [Abel G] by the multiplicative [*] notation even though it is later shown to be commutative. *)

(** Firstly we define the operation on [Abel G]. This is defined as follows:
<<
        abel_in x * abel_in y := abel_in (x * y)
>>
      But we need to also check that it preserves [ab_comm] in the appropriate way. *)

goals.conclusion: forall x y z : G, ?a (x * (y * z)) = ?a (x * (z * y))
forall x y z : G, ?a (x * (y * z)) = ?a (x * (z * y))

forall x y z : G,
(fun a : G => abel_in (a * b)) (x * (y * z)) =
(fun a : G => abel_in (a * b)) (x * (z * y))
abel_in (a * (c * d) * b) = abel_in (a * (d * c) * b)

contents: (* The pattern seems to be to alternate [associativity] and [ab_comm]. *)

goals.conclusion: abel_in (a * (c * d * b)) = abel_in (a * (d * c) * b)
abel_in (a * b * (c * d)) = abel_in (a * (d * c) * b)
abel_in (a * b * (d * c)) = abel_in (a * (d * c) * b)
abel_in (a * (d * c * b)) = abel_in (a * (d * c) * b)

forall x y z : G,
(fun b : G =>
 Abel_rec G (Abel G) (fun a0 : G => abel_in (a0 * b))
   (fun a0 c d : G =>
    ap abel_in (associativity a0 (c * d) b)^ @
    (abel_in_comm a0 (c * d) b @
     (ap abel_in (associativity a0 b (c * d)) @
      (abel_in_comm (a0 * b) c d @
       (ap abel_in (associativity a0 b (d * c))^ @
        (abel_in_comm a0 b (d * c) @ ap abel_in (associativity a0 (d * c) b))))))
    :
    (fun a1 : G => abel_in (a1 * b)) (a0 * (c * d)) =
    (fun a1 : G => abel_in (a1 * b)) (a0 * (d * c)))
   a)
  (x * (y * z)) =
(fun b : G =>
 Abel_rec G (Abel G) (fun a0 : G => abel_in (a0 * b))
   (fun a0 c d : G =>
    ap abel_in (associativity a0 (c * d) b)^ @
    (abel_in_comm a0 (c * d) b @
     (ap abel_in (associativity a0 b (c * d)) @
      (abel_in_comm (a0 * b) c d @
       (ap abel_in (associativity a0 b (d * c))^ @
        (abel_in_comm a0 b (d * c) @ ap abel_in (associativity a0 (d * c) b))))))
    :
    (fun a1 : G => abel_in (a1 * b)) (a0 * (c * d)) =
    (fun a1 : G => abel_in (a1 * b)) (a0 * (d * c)))
   a)
  (x * (z * y))

(fun b0 : G =>
 Abel_rec G (Abel G) (fun a0 : G => abel_in (a0 * b0))
   (fun a0 c0 d0 : G =>
    ap abel_in (associativity a0 (c0 * d0) b0)^ @
    (abel_in_comm a0 (c0 * d0) b0 @
     (ap abel_in (associativity a0 b0 (c0 * d0)) @
      (abel_in_comm (a0 * b0) c0 d0 @
       (ap abel_in (associativity a0 b0 (d0 * c0))^ @
        (abel_in_comm a0 b0 (d0 * c0) @
         ap abel_in (associativity a0 (d0 * c0) b0))))))
    :
    (fun a1 : G => abel_in (a1 * b0)) (a0 * (c0 * d0)) =
    (fun a1 : G => abel_in (a1 * b0)) (a0 * (d0 * c0)))
   a)
  (b * (c * d)) =
(fun b0 : G =>
 Abel_rec G (Abel G) (fun a0 : G => abel_in (a0 * b0))
   (fun a0 c0 d0 : G =>
    ap abel_in (associativity a0 (c0 * d0) b0)^ @
    (abel_in_comm a0 (c0 * d0) b0 @
     (ap abel_in (associativity a0 b0 (c0 * d0)) @
      (abel_in_comm (a0 * b0) c0 d0 @
       (ap abel_in (associativity a0 b0 (d0 * c0))^ @
        (abel_in_comm a0 b0 (d0 * c0) @
         ap abel_in (associativity a0 (d0 * c0) b0))))))
    :
    (fun a1 : G => abel_in (a1 * b0)) (a0 * (c0 * d0)) =
    (fun a1 : G => abel_in (a1 * b0)) (a0 * (d0 * c0)))
   a)
  (b * (d * c))

forall a : Abel G,
(fun b0 : G =>
 Abel_rec G (Abel G) (fun a0 : G => abel_in (a0 * b0))
   (fun a0 c0 d0 : G =>
    ap abel_in (associativity a0 (c0 * d0) b0)^ @
    (abel_in_comm a0 (c0 * d0) b0 @
     (ap abel_in (associativity a0 b0 (c0 * d0)) @
      (abel_in_comm (a0 * b0) c0 d0 @
       (ap abel_in (associativity a0 b0 (d0 * c0))^ @
        (abel_in_comm a0 b0 (d0 * c0) @
         ap abel_in (associativity a0 (d0 * c0) b0))))))
    :
    (fun a1 : G => abel_in (a1 * b0)) (a0 * (c0 * d0)) =
    (fun a1 : G => abel_in (a1 * b0)) (a0 * (d0 * c0)))
   a)
  (b * (c * d)) =
(fun b0 : G =>
 Abel_rec G (Abel G) (fun a0 : G => abel_in (a0 * b0))
   (fun a0 c0 d0 : G =>
    ap abel_in (associativity a0 (c0 * d0) b0)^ @
    (abel_in_comm a0 (c0 * d0) b0 @
     (ap abel_in (associativity a0 b0 (c0 * d0)) @
      (abel_in_comm (a0 * b0) c0 d0 @
       (ap abel_in (associativity a0 b0 (d0 * c0))^ @
        (abel_in_comm a0 b0 (d0 * c0) @
         ap abel_in (associativity a0 (d0 * c0) b0))))))
    :
    (fun a1 : G => abel_in (a1 * b0)) (a0 * (c0 * d0)) =
    (fun a1 : G => abel_in (a1 * b0)) (a0 * (d0 * c0)))
   a)
  (b * (d * c))

contents: (** We can now easily show that this operation is associative by [associativity] in [G] and the fact that being associative is a proposition. *)

goals.conclusion: forall z : Abel G, x * (y * z) = x * y * z
forall y : Abel G, (fun x0 : Abel G => x * (y * x0) = x * y * x0) (abel_in z)

forall x : Abel G,
(fun x0 : Abel G =>
 (fun x1 : Abel G => x * (x0 * x1) = x * x0 * x1) (abel_in z)) 
  (abel_in y)
abel_in x * (abel_in y * abel_in z) = abel_in x * abel_in y * abel_in z

contents: (** From this we know that [Abel G] is a semigroup. *)
(** We define the group unit as [abel_in] of the unit of [G] *)
#[export] Instance monunit_abel_zero : MonUnit (Abel G) := abel_in mon_unit.
(** By using Abel_ind_hprop we can prove the left and right identity laws. *)
(** We can also prove that the operation is commutative! This will come in handy later. *)

goals.conclusion: forall y : Abel G, x * y = y * x
(fun x0 : Abel G => x * x0 = x0 * x) (abel_in y)
forall x : Abel G, (fun x0 : Abel G => x * x0 = x0 * x) (abel_in y)
abel_in x * abel_in y = abel_in y * abel_in x
abel_in (1 * (x * y)) = abel_in y * abel_in x

contents:
(** Now we can define the inverse operation. This is just
<<
        (abel_in g)^ := abel_in g^
>>
      However when checking that it respects [ab_comm] we have to show the following:
<<
        abel_in (z^ * y^ * x^) = abel_in (y^ * z^ * x^)
>>
      there is no obvious way to do this, but we note that [abel_in (x * y)] is exactly the definition of [abel_in x * abel_in y]! Hence by [commutativity] we can show this. *)

goals.conclusion:
forall x y z : G,
(abel_in o inv) (x * (y * z)) = (abel_in o inv) (x * (z * y))

contents: intros x y z; cbn beta.
1,3: lhs rapply inverse_sg_op; napply (ap (.* _)); rapply inverse_sg_op.

goals.conclusion: abel_in (z^ * y^ * x^) = abel_in (y^ * z^ * x^)

contents:
change (abel_in z^ * abel_in y^ * abel_in x^
      = abel_in y^ * abel_in z^ * abel_in x^).

goals.conclusion: abel_in z^ * abel_in y^ * abel_in x^ = abel_in y^ * abel_in z^ * abel_in x^
abel_in z^ * abel_in y^ = abel_in y^ * abel_in z^

contents: (** Again by [Abel_ind_hprop] and the corresponding laws for [G] we can prove the left and right inverse laws. *)
(** And furthermore, since the operation is commutative, it is an abelian group. *)
(** By definition, the map [abel_in] is also a group homomorphism. *)
(** We can easily prove that [abel_in] is a surjection. *)

goals.conclusion: forall b : Abel G, merely (hfiber abel_in b)

contents:
(** Now we finally check that our definition of abelianization satisfies the universal property of being an abelianization. *)

(** We define [abel] to be the abelianization of a group. This is a map from [Group] to [AbGroup]. *)
(** We don't wish for [abel G] to be unfolded when using [simpl] or [cbn]. *)
(** The unit of this map is the map [abel_in] which typeclasses can pick up to be a homomorphism. We write it out explicitly here. *)

Definition abel_unit {G : Group} : G $-> abel G
  := @Build_GroupHomomorphism G (abel G) abel_in _.

Definition grp_homo_abel_rec {G : Group} {A : AbGroup} (f : G $-> A)
  : abel G $-> A.

goals.conclusion: forall x y z : G, f (x * (y * z)) = f (x * (z * y))
f y * f z = f z * f y

IsSemiGroupPreserving
  (Abel_rec G A f
     (fun x y z : G =>
      grp_homo_op_agree f f 1
        ((grp_homo_op f y z @ commutativity (f y) (f z)) @
         (grp_homo_op f z y)^)))

forall y0 : abel G,
Abel_rec G A f
  (fun x y1 z : G =>
   grp_homo_op_agree f f 1
     ((grp_homo_op f y1 z @ commutativity (f y1) (f z)) @
      (grp_homo_op f z y1)^))
  (y * y0) =
Abel_rec G A f
  (fun x y1 z : G =>
   grp_homo_op_agree f f 1
     ((grp_homo_op f y1 z @ commutativity (f y1) (f z)) @
      (grp_homo_op f z y1)^))
  y *
Abel_rec G A f
  (fun x y1 z : G =>
   grp_homo_op_agree f f 1
     ((grp_homo_op f y1 z @ commutativity (f y1) (f z)) @
      (grp_homo_op f z y1)^))
  y0

forall y : abel G,
(fun x0 : Abel G =>
 Abel_rec G A f
   (fun x1 y0 z : G =>
    grp_homo_op_agree f f 1
      ((grp_homo_op f y0 z @ commutativity (f y0) (f z)) @
       (grp_homo_op f z y0)^))
   (y * x0) =
 Abel_rec G A f
   (fun x1 y0 z : G =>
    grp_homo_op_agree f f 1
      ((grp_homo_op f y0 z @ commutativity (f y0) (f z)) @
       (grp_homo_op f z y0)^))
   y *
 Abel_rec G A f
   (fun x1 y0 z : G =>
    grp_homo_op_agree f f 1
      ((grp_homo_op f y0 z @ commutativity (f y0) (f z)) @
       (grp_homo_op f z y0)^))
   x0)
  (abel_in x)

(fun x0 : Abel G =>
 (fun x1 : Abel G =>
  Abel_rec G A f
    (fun x2 y0 z : G =>
     grp_homo_op_agree f f 1
       ((grp_homo_op f y0 z @ commutativity (f y0) (f z)) @
        (grp_homo_op f z y0)^))
    (x0 * x1) =
  Abel_rec G A f
    (fun x2 y0 z : G =>
     grp_homo_op_agree f f 1
       ((grp_homo_op f y0 z @ commutativity (f y0) (f z)) @
        (grp_homo_op f z y0)^))
    x0 *
  Abel_rec G A f
    (fun x2 y0 z : G =>
     grp_homo_op_agree f f 1
       ((grp_homo_op f y0 z @ commutativity (f y0) (f z)) @
        (grp_homo_op f z y0)^))
    x1)
   (abel_in x))
  (abel_in y)
forall x : G, f (abel_in x) = g (abel_in x)

contents: (** Finally we can prove that our construction abel is an abelianization. *)

Instance isabelianization_abel {G : Group}
  : IsAbelianization (abel G) abel_unit.

goals.conclusion:
forall x y : abel G $-> A,
group_precomp A abel_unit x $== group_precomp A abel_unit y -> x $== y
{a : abel G $-> A & group_precomp A abel_unit a $== h}

(fun a : abel G $-> A => group_precomp A abel_unit a $== h)
  (grp_homo_abel_rec h)

(fun x : G =>
 Coeq_ind
   (fun
      _ : Coeq (uncurry2 (fun a b c : G => a * (b * c)))
            (uncurry2 (fun a b c : G => a * (c * b))) =>
    A)
   h
   (fun b : G * G * G =>
    transport_compose
      (fun
         _ : Trunc 0
               (Coeq (uncurry2 (fun a b0 c : G => a * (b0 * c)))
                  (uncurry2 (fun a b0 c : G => a * (c * b0)))) =>
       A)
      tr (cglue (fst (fst b), snd (fst b), snd b))
      (h
         (uncurry2 (fun a b0 c : G => a * (b0 * c))
            (fst (fst b), snd (fst b), snd b))) @
    dp_const
      (grp_homo_op_agree h h 1
         ((grp_homo_op h (snd (fst b)) (snd b) @
           commutativity (h (snd (fst b))) (h (snd b))) @
          (grp_homo_op h (snd b) (snd (fst b)))^)))
   (coeq x)) ==
h

forall x y : abel G $-> A,
group_precomp A abel_unit x $== group_precomp A abel_unit y -> x $== y

hypotheses.type: group_precomp A abel_unit g $== group_precomp A abel_unit h

goals.conclusion:
(fun x0 : Abel G =>
 (grp_homo_map : GroupHomomorphism (abel G) A -> abel G $-> A) g x0 =
 (grp_homo_map : GroupHomomorphism (abel G) A -> abel G $-> A) h x0)
  (abel_in x)

hypotheses.type: group_precomp A abel_unit g $== group_precomp A abel_unit h

contents: destruct (esssurj (group_precomp B eta1) eta2) as [a ac].
destruct (esssurj (group_precomp A eta2) eta1) as [b bc].

refine (essinj (group_precomp B eta2)
                   (x := a $o b) (y := Id (A := Group) B) _).

goals.conclusion: group_precomp B eta2 (a $o b) $== group_precomp B eta2 (Id B)

contents:
refine (essinj (group_precomp A eta1)
                   (x := b $o a) (y := Id (A := Group) A) _).

goals.conclusion: group_precomp A eta1 (b $o a) $== group_precomp A eta1 (Id A)

contents:
Theorem homotopic_isabelianization {G : Group} (A B : AbGroup)
  (eta1 : GroupHomomorphism G A) (eta2 : GroupHomomorphism G B)
  {isab1 : IsAbelianization A eta1} {isab2 : IsAbelianization B eta2}
  : eta2 == grp_homo_compose (groupiso_isabelianization A B eta1 eta2) eta1.

goals.conclusion: eta2 == grp_homo_compose (groupiso_isabelianization A B eta1 eta2) eta1
eta2 == grp_homo_compose (groupiso_isabelianization A B eta1 eta2) eta1
eta2 x = grp_homo_compose (groupiso_isabelianization A B eta1 eta2) eta1 x
grp_homo_compose (groupiso_isabelianization A B eta1 eta2) eta1 x = eta2 x

contents: pose (homotopic_isabelianization A (abel G) eta abel_unit) as p.

hypotheses.type:
abel_unit ==
grp_homo_compose (groupiso_isabelianization A (abel G) eta abel_unit) eta

contents: Instance isabelianization_identity (A : AbGroup) : IsAbelianization A grp_homo_id.

goals.conclusion:
forall x y : A $-> B,
group_precomp B grp_homo_id x $== group_precomp B grp_homo_id y -> x $== y

forall x y : A $-> B,
group_precomp B grp_homo_id x $== group_precomp B grp_homo_id y -> x $== y

contents: intros g h p; exact p.

goals.conclusion: groupiso_isabelianization A B grp_homo_id eta == eta
forall a b : Group, (a $-> b) -> abel a $-> abel b
forall (a b : Group) (f g : a $-> b), f $== g -> fmap abel f $== fmap abel g
forall a : Group, fmap abel (Id a) $== Id (abel a)

forall (a b c : Group) (f : a $-> b) (g : b $-> c),
fmap abel (g $o f) $== fmap abel g $o fmap abel f
forall (a b : Group) (f g : a $-> b), f $== g -> fmap abel f $== fmap abel g

contents: intros A B f g p.

goals.conclusion: fmap abel f $== fmap abel g
fmap abel f $== fmap abel g
forall x : A, fmap abel f (abel_in x) = fmap abel g (abel_in x)
fmap abel f (abel_in x) = fmap abel g (abel_in x)
forall a : Group, fmap abel (Id a) $== Id (abel a)

forall (a b c : Group) (f : a $-> b) (g : b $-> c),
fmap abel (g $o f) $== fmap abel g $o fmap abel f

contents: intros A B C f g.

goals.conclusion: fmap abel (g $o f) $== fmap abel g $o fmap abel f
